Capabilities of the Techtiz SLED Backbone

What we bring on day one.

A reference page a prime can scan in two minutes to confirm Techtiz can plug into the bid stack.

Download capability statementStart an NDA conversation →
Procurement & registration
NAICS Codes
541511 · 541512 · 541519
Custom Computer Programming · Computer Systems Design
NIGP Codes
918-46 · 918-00
Software Development · Technology
Insurance
Per engagement
Routed through prime master agreement
Compliance frameworks

What a prime compliance officer is looking for.

Specific framework claims with status, scope, and target dates. We don't list controls we can't substantiate. Where a framework is in-progress, the target is on the page.

See the full operating model and architecture →
SOC 2 Type II
In progress
Operational cybersecurity controls. Independent audit of access, change management, monitoring, and incident response across a 6–12 month observation window.
Target
Q4 2026 · Bridge letter on request
CJIS Security Policy
Posture
Required for LE, court, and dispatch work. Fingerprinted, background-checked U.S. personnel on U.S. soil for any CJI touch. Offshore plane never touches CJI.
Evidence
Personnel attestations to the prime's CJIS coordinator
StateRAMP / TX-RAMP
Aligned
StateRAMP-aware cloud architecture. We design inside the prime's authorization boundary using controls that map cleanly to StateRAMP and TX-RAMP categorization.
Scope
Architecture, SSP narrative, 3PAO response support
Section 508 / VPAT
Applied
Required for all government digital interfaces. WCAG 2.2 AA validation, screen-reader regression, PDF/UA conversion, plain-language editing. VPAT 2.5 ITI authored per surface.
Deliverable
Signed VPAT ships with every scoped engagement
NIST 800-171 / CMMC
Aligned
Baseline for primes serving federal customers. 110-control baseline implemented across access, audit, configuration, incident response, and media protection.
Scope
Self-assessment · SSP & POAM maintained · CMMC Level 2 alignment
HIPAA · IRS Pub 1075
Posture
For healthcare, eligibility, and tax-data work. BAA executed as sub under prime BAA. FTI and PHI handled by U.S. personnel on U.S. soil per Pub 1075 §9.3.
Evidence
Onshore-only for any PHI / FTI / agency PII touch
Capability matrix

Eight rows. One per Backbone component.

Mapped by depth, not breadth. Each row is something we've shipped on a real engagement.

Capability
What we do
Tools we use
Regulatory & Statutory Compliance
What we do Plan-content checklists, statute-citation validation, adoption-sequence tracking against state framework guidance.
Tools Custom rule engines · Statutory framework libraries · EGLE guidance maps
GIS & Data Pipelines
What we do Facility inventories, forecast models, supply chain analysis, parcel data, multi-source spatial joins.
Tools ArcGIS · PostGIS · GeoPandas · Mapbox · Leaflet
Public Engagement Technology
What we do Multilingual surveys, comment capture, disadvantaged-community reach, hearing presentation packs.
Tools Custom survey platforms · Accessibility tooling · i18n stacks · Plain-language pipelines
Grant Reporting Automation
What we do Quarterly milestone reports auto-generated, reconciliation against delivery data, federal-state report alignment.
Tools Make.com · n8n · Custom reporting pipelines · Workday Adaptive
Document Production & Format Compliance
What we do Statutory-format compliant deliverables in PDF, InDesign, ArcGIS, Word, Excel. PDF/UA accessibility built in.
Tools Adobe Creative Suite · Microsoft Office · PDF/UA tooling · LaTeX
Accessibility & Equity
What we do WCAG 2.2 AA validation, multilingual content, disadvantaged-community engagement, plain-language editing.
Tools axe DevTools · Screen-reader testing (NVDA, VoiceOver) · Plain-language style guides
Adoption Sequence Tracking
What we do Statutory comment periods, public hearings, board adoption, EGLE submission, deadline alerting.
Tools Project management instrumentation · Notion · Linear · Custom deadline alerting
Audit Trail & Records Management
What we do Every delivery action timestamped, attributable, exportable for FOIA and state public-records requests.
Tools Custom audit logging · Structured records export · S3 immutable storage
Tech stack

Four quadrants of working tools.

GIS

Spatial & data
ArcGIS Pro ArcGIS Online PostGIS GeoPandas Mapbox Leaflet QGIS Turf.js

Engagement & Automation

Public-facing workflows
Make.com n8n Zapier Custom survey platforms SurveyMonkey API Twilio SendGrid i18next

Docs & Publishing

Statutory-format deliverables
Adobe InDesign Adobe Acrobat Pro Microsoft Office PDF/UA LaTeX Pandoc Word XML

Data & Reporting

Audit, grant, compliance
PostgreSQL Supabase Metabase DuckDB dbt Airbyte Workday Adaptive S3
What we don't do

Honest about the lane.

Trust comes from knowing where the work ends. These six categories belong to the prime, to a specialist sub, to dedicated capture-consulting firms, or to nobody at all.

✕

Fieldwork

Site visits, in-person stakeholder facilitation, on-site meeting moderation. We support remotely; the prime owns the physical room.

✕

Legal drafting

Legal counsel for statutory interpretation must come from the prime or a separate sub. We follow the legal opinion; we don't issue it.

✕

Prime contractor role

We don't bid against our partners. Subcontract-only. Direct-to-agency engagements are out of scope.

✕

Direct-to-agency engagements

Every engagement routes through a U.S. prime under a master subcontract. NDAs and IP through prime templates.

✕

Off-the-shelf product replacement

We don't compete with Tyler, Granicus, CivicPlus, OpenGov, GovPilot or other GovTech product companies. When a prime needs a product, we recommend the right one. We're the subcontractor that ships the custom piece the off-the-shelf platform doesn't cover.

✕

Procurement intelligence / capture-consulting analyst services

We do not sell access to public RFPs, anonymized tenders, or procurement subscription data. That lane is operated by Deltek GovWin, Bloomberg Government, and dedicated U.S. capture-consulting firms with analyst teams and regulatory structures. We are a technical implementation subcontractor that supports primes inside structured engagements.

How we engage

Every engagement begins with a structured relationship.

NDA on first substantive contact. No public materials exchanged before paperwork is signed. We do not solicit RFPs from the open market or analyze RFPs we have not been formally engaged on.

01
Day 1 to 5

Introduction

Email or LinkedIn from a capture manager, BD lead, or program manager at a U.S. prime. Within one business day, a Techtiz partner manager replies with a mutual NDA template and a calendar slot.

02
Week 1 to 2

Capability conversation under NDA

30 to 60 minute call. The prime describes the engagement type, the procurement vehicle, and any sensitive context. We describe which Backbone capabilities apply, the team shape, and the contracting structure.

03
Week 2 to 4

Scoped subcontract conversation

If the engagement fits, we deliver a scoped statement of work, teaming letter draft, and named-resume options. Your contracts team takes it from there. Subcontract paperwork closes within 7 to 14 days for most vehicles.

Engage us

Considering a teaming arrangement?

Start an NDA conversation. Engagement structure proposed within 5 business days of NDA.

Talk to us about a subcontract role Capability statement →
